返回日报
Hacker News #8

一层就够了吗？单层Transformer即可媲美全参数强化学习训练

Is One Layer Enough? A Single Transformer Layer Matches Full-Parameter RL Train

AI 作者 tcp_handshaker 热度 95 回复 22 2026/07/02 20:10
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • View PDF HTML (experimental) Abstract:Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a central component of post-training large language models (LLMs), yet little is understood about how RL adaptation is distributed across transformer layers. Existing approaches typically update all model parameters uniformly, implicitly assuming that every layer contributes similarly to the gains obtained during RL post-training. In this work, we challenge this assumption through a systematic layer-wise study of RL training. Surprisingly, we find that training a single transformer layer can recover most of the gains achieved by full-parameter RL training, and in some cases even surpass it. To quantify this phenomenon, we introduce the quantity layer contribution, which measures the fraction of full RL improvement recovered by training a layer in isolation. Across seven models spanning two model families (Qwen3, Qwen2.5), three RL algorithms (GRPO, GiGPO, Dr. GRPO), and multiple task domains including mathematical reasoning, code generation, and agentic decision-making, we observe a remarkably stable pattern: RL gains are highly concentrated in a small subset of, and in many cases even a single, transformer layers. More strikingly, the same structural pattern consistently emerges: high-contribution layers concentrate in the middle of the transformer stack, while layers near the input and output ends contribute substantially less. The resulting layer rankings remain strongly correlated across datasets, tasks, model families, and RL algorithms.
  • Subjects: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL) Cite as: arXiv:2607.01232 [cs.LG] (or arXiv:2607.01232v1 [cs.LG] for this version) https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2607.01232 arXiv-issued DOI via DataCite (pending registration) Submission history From: Zijian Zhang [view email] [v1] Wed, 1 Jul 2026 17:59:54 UTC (268 KB)

命中 4 个 AI/科技关键词。

正文翻译

暂无正文

暂无原文

回复翻译 20

HarHarVeryFunny 2026/07/02 22:51
有趣的是，受RL后训练影响最大的是Transformer的中间层，但考虑到RL被用来塑造输出中高层规划类型的方向，这在直觉上或许也说得通。

Transformer的输入层似乎必然要执行最底层的语法到语义增强工作，从词性标注等任务开始。同样，输出层也必然要负责将高层表示映射回表层词序列形式。这就使得中间层需要完成以下工作：首先识别足够深层的模式以支持高质量的预测，然后进行高层预测本身，而这正是RL通常试图塑造的部分。

It's interesting that it's the middle layers of the Transformer that are affected most by RL post-training, but it perhaps makes some intuitive sense given that RL is being used to shape high level planning-type direction of the output.

It seems that the input layers to a Transformer are necessarily going to be doing the most low level work of syntax -> semantic augmentation starting with things like tagging parts of speech etc. Similarly the output layers are by necessity going to be concerned with mapping high level representations back into surface level word sequence form. This leaves the middle layers to do the work of first recognizing deep enough patterns to support good quality prediction, then do the high level predication itself which is what RL is typically going to be trying to shape.

楼层
mike_hearn 2026/07/02 22:29
这个结果让人感觉非常直观。Transformer的早期层可以被理解为理解表层事物，如语法、token如何分组、哪些组是实体以及如何消歧等。最后一层在某种意义上是将思想解码为词语的选择，确保语法合理、文本流畅且结构正确等。中间层则是抽象思维和概念操作发生的地方。

但对于本文用于RL训练的任务来说，其核心在于改善网络操作概念的方式。因此，中间层才是重点所在。

注：RL也用于非概念操作的任务，如指令微调。我敢打赌他们的结果在那种情况下不成立，因为与基础模型相比，差异完全在于词语的选择和文本的流畅度，而不是核心理解。

This result feels very intuitive. The early layers of a transformer can be thought of as understanding surface level things like syntax, how tokens group, which groups are entities and how to disambiguate them, etc. The last layers are in a sense decoding ideas into a selection of words, ensuring the grammar makes sense, that the text flows and is structured correctly, etc. The middle layers are where the abstract thought and manipulation of concepts is happening.

But for the tasks this paper uses for RL training, it's all about improving the way the net is manipulating concepts. So the middle layers are where the focus should be.

Note: RL is also used for tasks that aren't about conceptual manipulation, like instruct training. I bet that their result doesn't hold for that because the delta vs the foundation model is all about the selection of words and flow of the text, not the core understanding.

楼层
throw310822 2026/07/03 00:35
我一直在想RYS（重复你自己）实验，它只是简单地循环LLM的一些内部层以获得更好的结果，不知道这方面有没有取得什么进展。

https://dnhkng.github.io/posts/rys/

感觉在LLM中集成一个控制循环的网络应该是很直接的。或者干脆在初始训练后直接复制整个层块。

I keep thinking of the RYS (Repeat Yourself) experiment of simply looping some of the inner layers of LLMs for better results and wonder if any progress was made on it.

https://dnhkng.github.io/posts/rys/

Feels it should be straightforward to integrate in LLMs a network to control the looping. Or just duplicate entire blocks of layers after the initial training.

楼层
olliepro 2026/07/02 23:06
作者在训练token长度方面存在一些不一致之处……

大多数错误可能是回复在达到3K token限制前未完成。他们测量了RL将回复缩短至其限制范围内的效果。

The authors have some inconsistencies with training token length…

Most errors are probably responses that didn’t finish before their 3K token limit. They’ve measured how well RL is able to shorten the response to their limit.

楼层
usernametaken29 2026/07/02 21:10
如果你仔细思考一段时间，就会意识到Transformer本质上是强化版的自编码器。一个小的输入空间被扩展到一个大的流形上，然后再收缩。 现在，假设你想施加一个函数来调节自编码器的输出。其实很明显，你只需要恰好一层来实现这一点……f(流形)。
If you think about it for some time then you’ll come to realise transformers are autoencoders on steroids. A small input space is expanded onto a big manifold and contracted again. Now, suppose you want to impose a function to regulate the output of an autoencoder. It’s actually pretty obvious that you need exactly one layer to do so… f(manifold).
楼层
sailingparrot 2026/07/03 00:37
一切都可以表示为f()，一个全规模的最先进Transformer模型也不过是f(context)。这并不意味着一层就足够了。这完全取决于这个f要成为一个好模型所需的表达能力水平。
Everything can be represented as f(), a full scale SotA transformer model is also just f(context). That does not mean one layer is sufficient. It all depends on the level of expressivity required by this f to be a good model.
楼层
getnormality 2026/07/02 22:04
你的建议似乎不可思议地远远超出了论文所说的内容。

RL后训练会改变Transformer的参数，而你的f(流形)想法似乎暗示只需在顶部加一个新层就够了，根本不需要改变Transformer本身。

如果真是这样那就太方便了，但我猜并非如此，否则这早就成为主流方法了。

What you're suggesting seems to go implausibly far beyond what the paper says.

RL post-training alters the parameters of the transformer, while your f(manifold) idea seems to suggest that a new layer on top would suffice, no need to alter the transformer itself at all.

It would be extremely handy if that were so, but I'm guessing it isn't, or it would be the prevailing approach.

楼层
wrs 2026/07/03 00:32
流形在中间（“小输入空间被扩展到一个大流形上，然后再次收缩”），所以f(流形)也需要在中间。
The manifold is in the middle (“small input space is expanded onto a big manifold and contracted again”) so f(manifold) would need to be in the middle too.
楼层
earthnail 2026/07/02 21:20
我花了一点时间才理解你说的“强化版自编码器”是什么意思，但我认为你指的是具有反向瓶颈的自编码器——其中间表示不是更小，而是比输入空间大得多。我对您评论的理解正确吗？
Took me a short time to understand what you mean with "autoencoders on steroids", but I believe you mean they are autoencoders with an inverse bottleneck - an intermediate representation that isn't smaller, but that's much larger than the input space. Is my understanding of your comment correct?
楼层
usernametaken29 2026/07/02 21:33
差不多。自编码器的嵌入不需要比输入小。它们唯一的要求是压缩信息，从而产生重构损失。然而，通常它们不会以这种方式训练，因为它们无法收敛。Transformer做的是同样的事情，但由于其设计方式，它们可以在一次前向传播中传递多得多的信息位。这对于仅解码器网络也成立，因为它们仍然在做同样的事情。
Kind of. Autoencoders don’t need to have an embedding that’s smaller than the input. Their only requirement is that they compress information and thus create reconstruction loss. Typically however they are not trained this way because they don’t converge.. transformers do the same thing, but they can squeeze much more bits of information through one pass because the way they are designed. This holds true even for decoder only networks because they’re still doing the same thing
楼层
soraki_soladead 2026/07/02 21:22
我可能误解了你的观点，但这混淆了它们各自的显著特征。你提到了扩展，但自编码器在经典意义上是压缩其输入的。自编码器有明确的编码器和解码器。我们现在使用的大多数Transformer（LLM）都是仅解码器架构。流形通常不是模型直接应用的对象。我们将函数/模型应用于潜在表示。那些才是存在于流形上的东西。
I might be misunderstanding your point but this conflates the distinguishing features of each. you mention expansion but autoencoders canonically compress their inputs. autoencoders have an explicit encoder and decoder. most transformers we interact with these days (LLMs) are decoder only. the manifold isn't typically something the model is applied to directly. we apply the function/model to the latent representations. those are what live on the manifold.
楼层
usernametaken29 2026/07/02 21:53
这挺有意思的……潜在表示和流形究竟有什么区别？在我看来，它们是一回事，你只是在构建流形本身的分段函数。解码器也以非常相似的方式生成流形，区别在于编码器不是通过学习得到的，而是在初始化后保持静态。所以从根本上说，它执行的仍然是相同的操作。
Now that’s interesting.. what exactly distinguishes latent representations and the manifold? IMHO, those are the same, and you’re constructing a piecewise function of the manifold itself. Decoders also produce manifolds much in the same way, with the distinction being that the encoder isn’t learned but static after initialisation. So fundamentally it is still DOING the same operation.
楼层
soraki_soladead 2026/07/02 22:10
数据的潜在表示就像曲面上的点。那个曲面就是流形。我们通常无法获得完整的流形，只能通过将数据嵌入其中来从中采样点。

值得注意的是，每次变换（例如每一层）之后都会“存在”一个不同的流形。只有当你应用相同的变换时，才会从同一个流形中采样。

The latent representations of the data are like points on a surface. That surface is the manifold. We don't typically have the full manifold and can only sample points from it by embedding data into it.

Worth noting a different manifold "exists" after each transformation (e.g. layer). You only sample from the same manifold when you apply the same transformation(s).

楼层
CuriouslyC 2026/07/02 22:49
同样值得注意的是，在现实中，流形在极高维度下会呈现“多刺”的形态，因此“曲面”的概念最好通过嵌入空间中样本之间的距离模式以及它们在低维空间中的坍缩方式来理解。
Also worth noting that in reality manifolds will be "spiky" in very high dimension, so the idea of a "surface" is best understood through patterns of distance between samples in embedding space and way they collapse in low D.
楼层
baq 2026/07/02 23:05

soleveloper 2026/07/02 22:49
有道理——这与在编码器-解码器架构（类似Bert风格）中微调下游任务非常相似
Makes sense - This is very similar to fine tuning a down stream task in encoder-decoder architecture (~Bert style)

khalic 2026/07/03 01:08
这项工作做得非常出色，太棒了
Really good work here, bravo
楼层
tribal808 2026/07/02 22:35
如果大部分性能提升都隐藏在少数几个中间层中，那么冻结其余层可以节省大量的计算资源
If most of the performance gains are hidden in a few middle layers, you can save a massive amount of compute by freezing the rest
楼层
